commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 150s Codex dynamic-tool watchdog for `skill_workshop` apply/reject/quarantine calls on the main and /btw paths, while adding regression tests that keep non-lifecycle calls at the 90s default.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +128. Total +150 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source shows a 90s Codex dynamic-tool default racing a 120s plugin approval wait plus gateway grace, and the existing before-tool-call test shows hooks can rewrite Skill Workshop params before final approval.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can address the timeout-selection gap and tests without changing Skill Workshop policy, config, docs, or plugin API surface.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): bound lifecycle approval timeout below Codex dynamic-tool watchdog
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `skill_workshop` lifecycle tool calls (apply/reject/quarantine) invoked from a Codex agent session fail with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop" before the operator approval prompt can resolve. The proposal is left pending and unmodified. The same failure occurs when the call is made from a `/btw` side thread.
- **Root Cause**: The Codex dynamic-tool bridge wraps every tool call with a per-tool watchdog (`CODEX_DYNAMIC_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000`, `dynamic-tool-execution.ts`). Skill Workshop lifecycle calls return a `requireApproval` payload that can wait up to `DEFAULT_PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` plus 10s gateway grace (130s total, `plugin-approvals.ts`, `agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`). The 90s watchdog fires first, surfacing a generic bridge timeout instead of a clean approval denial; the approval is never completed. The side-question path (`side-question.ts`) has its own independent timeout resolver with the same gap.
- **Fix**: Add `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` to `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs` (main thread) and `CODEX_SIDE_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` to `resolveSideDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` (side thread). Both resolvers check `args.action` against the lifecycle set (`apply`, `reject`, `quarantine`) before returning 150s, so non-lifecycle calls (`list`, `inspect`) still use the 90s default. The 150s deadline exceeds the 130s approval ceiling, so the approval can now complete or expire cleanly before the watchdog fires. `SKILL_WORKSHOP_LIFECYCLE_ACTIONS` is exported from `dynamic-tool-execution.ts` and imported in `side-question.ts` to keep both paths in sync.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.ts` — exported `CODEX_DYNAMIC_SKILL_WORKSHOP_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` and `SKILL_WORKSHOP_LIFECYCLE_ACTIONS`; `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs` gains an `args` parameter and returns 150s only for lifecycle actions.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts` — imports `SKILL_WORKSHOP_LIFECYCLE_ACTIONS` from `dynamic-tool-execution.ts`; `resolveSideDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` gains a `skill_workshop` lifecycle branch returning 150s.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts` — extended lifecycle test covers all three actions; added non-lifecycle (list/inspect) test confirming 90s default; fake-timer before/after proof unchanged.
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts` — two new tests: lifecycle actions get 150s; non-lifecycle get 90s.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Skill Workshop approval policy (`src/skills/workshop/policy.ts`) unchanged; the 120s approval window is preserved for all callers.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts`, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Skill Workshop with default `approvalPolicy: pending`.
2. Start a Codex agent session and prompt it to apply a pending skill proposal.
3. The agent invokes `skill_workshop(action="apply", proposal_id="...")`.
4. **Before this PR**: the Codex dynamic-tool watchdog fires at 90s with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop"; the approval prompt is never shown; the proposal stays pending.
5. **After this PR**: the 150s Codex deadline lets the approval flow reach its natural 120s expiry; if the operator does not respond the approval times out with a clean denial; if the operator responds the apply proceeds normally.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#91266)**: `skill_workshop(action="apply/reject/quarantine")` from a Codex agent session or `/btw` side thread no longer surfaces a generic 90s dynamic-tool timeout. Non-lifecycle calls (list, inspect) are unaffected and still use the 90s default.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with fake timers against `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout`, `resolveDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs`, and `resolveSideDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs`, the production functions on the timed-out paths)**: fake-timer tests drive `handleDynamicToolCallWithTimeout` directly with a mock `toolBridge` that simulates the approval wait duration. Unit tests cover all three lifecycle actions and confirm non-lifecycle actions still get 90s on both the main and side-question paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  42 passed (42)
```

New tests covering: (1) all three lifecycle actions return 150s on the main-thread path; (2) non-lifecycle `skill_workshop` calls (list, inspect, no action) return 90s on the main-thread path; (3) fake-timer proof that 90s watchdog fires before a 120s approval can resolve (before state); (4) fake-timer proof that a 120s approval completes within the 150s deadline (after state); (5) all three lifecycle actions return 150s on the side-thread path; (6) non-lifecycle `skill_workshop` calls return 90s on the side-thread path.

**Observed result after fix**: lifecycle approval calls get the 150s window on both the main-thread and `/btw` side-thread paths; non-lifecycle calls (list, inspect) stay at the 90s default on both paths; the `SKILL_WORKSHOP_LIFECYCLE_ACTIONS` set is defined once and imported by the side-question path so divergence if a new action is added is eliminated.

**What was not tested**: live Gateway + Codex agent session with an active operator approval prompt in the UI.

**Repro confirmation**: the before test fails if `timeoutMs` is raised to 150_000 (approval would complete); the after test fails if `timeoutMs` is lowered to 90_000 (watchdog fires at 90s before the 120s approval). The two tests together pin the exact boundary the fix crosses.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The 150s lifetime extension only applies to lifecycle actions. **Mitigation**: action-aware check in both `readConfiguredDynamicToolTimeoutMs` and `resolveSideDynamicToolCallTimeoutMs` ensures `list`/`inspect` calls still use the 90s default; per-call `timeoutMs` overrides still take precedence.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Codex extension

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91266
target.author: openperf
